name: Millhaven
region: Northern Lordaeron, south of Thalassian Pass, at a river bend on the road toward Corin's Crossing and Andorhal

sensoryProfile.sight: Open doors, cold chimneys, a dead mill wheel over the race, a squat granary with spilled barley, meals left out, blankets thrown back, and no bodies where the life of the village should have left them.
sensoryProfile.sound: No dogs, no livestock, no voices, only the river under the pilings, an occasional hinge in the wind, and the party's own movement made too loud by the rest of the silence.
sensoryProfile.smell: River damp, stale interiors, old grease, mold, dust, and the wrong sweet rot of plague-saturated domestic spaces.
sensoryProfile.touch: Cold latch iron, packed dirt, snapped crust in the kitchen plots, damp cellar stone, and mud churned soft by too many dragging feet.
sensoryProfile.atmosphere: Not a ruined village but an interrupted one — ordinary life frozen mid-motion and emptied so completely that the absence itself becomes evidence.

establishedFacts.fact: Millhaven is the first major surface convergence where Jaina expects the contamination to show visibly rather than only through magical reading.
The village sits by a river bend and includes at least a mill and a granary.
By the time the party reaches visual range, its chimneys are cold and the village appears abandoned or dead from a distance.
Thirty-six households show interrupted daily life without any bodies left behind.
The whole village is contaminated: well water, root cellar floors, troughs, and grain stores all read wrong, with the granary strongest but not yet proven as first source.
Many of Millhaven's dead walked out of the village and left a north-northeast spoor toward the Quel'Thalas road.
One survivor, Miri, was found uncontaminated in a cellar below the taint line.
By chapter4 Millhaven functions as the proof Sylvanas uses to break denial in the next infected village; it is now the benchmark disaster every southern settlement fears becoming.

eventsHere.event: The party reaches visual approach and confirms Millhaven as the tightest visible knot of wrongness so far.
Sylvanas orders a full sweep and the empty village becomes a working investigation site rather than a distant omen.
Jaina confirms the saturation runs through the entire village, while Sylvanas and Cyndia establish that many of the dead walked north.
Miri is recovered alive from a cellar and her testimony helps confirm the northbound movement of the undead.
Sylvanas dispatches Neral and the unnamed escort ranger north from Millhaven with warning and survivor, then continues south with the remaining four riders.
Millhaven is invoked as the concrete warning that wins access and urgency in the next living village on the road south.
